name: B-Tree with Range Queries
description: Implement a B-tree across two modules. Module btree_node.py must define: BTreeNode class with attributes keys (list[Any], sorted), values (list[Any]), children (list[BTreeNode]), is_leaf (bool); helpers is_full(min_degree) and split(min_degree) returning (median_key, median_value, right_node). Module btree.py must import from btree_node.py and implement BTree class with constructor BTree(min_degree: int = 3) and methods: insert(key, value); search(key) returns value or None; delete(key) returns True if removed; range_query(low, high) returns sorted list of (key, value) pairs with low <= key <= high; size() returns total entries; height() returns tree height (1 for single root). Keys must support < comparison.
signature:
# btree_node.py
class BTreeNode:
    def __init__(self, is_leaf: bool = True): ...
    def is_full(self, min_degree: int) -> bool: ...
    def split(self, min_degree: int): ...

# btree.py
from btree_node import BTreeNode

class BTree:
    def __init__(self, min_degree: int = 3): ...
    def insert(self, key, value) -> None: ...
    def search(self, key): ...
    def delete(self, key) -> bool: ...
    def range_query(self, low, high) -> list: ...
    def size(self) -> int: ...
    def height(self) -> int: ...
tests:
def test_basic_insert_search():
    t = BTree()
    t.insert(10, 'ten')
    assert t.search(10) == 'ten'

def test_size_grows():
    t = BTree()
    assert t.size() == 0
    t.insert(1, 'a')
    t.insert(2, 'b')
    assert t.size() == 2

def test_update_existing():
    t = BTree()
    t.insert(5, 'old')
    t.insert(5, 'new')
    assert t.search(5) == 'new'
    assert t.size() == 1

def test_range_query_basic():
    t = BTree()
    for k in [5, 1, 8, 3, 10, 12, 2]:
        t.insert(k, str(k))
    result = t.range_query(2, 8)
    keys = [k for k, v in result]
    assert keys == [2, 3, 5, 8]

def test_delete_existing():
    t = BTree()
    t.insert(5, 'a')
    t.insert(10, 'b')
    assert t.delete(5) is True
    assert t.search(5) is None
    assert t.size() == 1

def test_delete_missing():
    t = BTree()
    t.insert(1, 'a')
    assert t.delete(99) is False
constraints: min_degree (t) >= 2; nodes have at least t-1 keys (except root) and at most 2t-1
Keys within a node remain sorted
search returns None for missing keys
Re-inserting an existing key updates its value without growing size
range_query returns pairs in ascending key order
height of empty tree is 0; tree with single root is 1
